hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'rex' would be 'regis' — 'rex' is the nominative form
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'rex' would be 'regem' — 'rex' is the nominative form
hints.Vocative: Vocative of 'rex' is the same as nominative — but here 'rex' functions as a predicate nominative with 'Tu'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'regis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'regis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'regis' ends in -is

hints.Nominative: Nominative singular is 'gloria' — 'gloriae' with -ae ending is genitive singular here
hints.Dative: While 'gloriae' could be dative, here it modifies 'rex' as a genitive of description
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'gloria' would be 'gloria' — 'gloriae' has the -ae genitive ending

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'gloriae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'gloriae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns have genitive in -ei — 'gloriae' ends in -ae

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Christus' — 'Christe' has the -e vocative ending of 2nd declension
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'Christus' would be 'Christi' — 'Christe' is the vocative form
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'Christus' would be 'Christum' — 'Christe' is the vocative form

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'Christi' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'Christi' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'Christi' ends in -i

translation: You are the King of glory, O Christ
